_README: ACQUIRE recipe registry — the source_id -> deterministic re-ingest map the heartbeat needs.
onboard.py has NO per-source-id re-ingest entrypoint (it matches only 37 human queue names and
re-runs full LLM recon+codegen every time), so the heartbeat NEVER auto-ingests a source unless it
has an ENABLED recipe here. Adding (and enabling) a source below is the explicit OPT-IN act.
enabled:           bool. false => heartbeat SKIPS the source and surfaces 'needs a human'.
kind:              'script' (a deterministic LLM-free loader in scripts/) or 'onboard' (LLM path).
cmd:               argv. cmd[0] ending in .py is resolved against the repo root + run with python3.
Tokens {TODAY} {TOMORROW} {YESTERDAY} expand at run time (ISO dates).
cost_estimate_cr:  rough credit cost. heartbeat refuses the source if spendable < 2x this.
note:              why it is (or isn't) safe to automate.
SAFE recipes are LLM-free, snapshot-replace (idempotent), and SHA-skip an unchanged reload.
Do NOT enable scrape/auth/incremental-append/huge-chunked sources here without a human review —
those are the tiers that double-append, brittle-break, or blow the budget.

fed_cisa_kev.note: VERIFIED SAFE: single CC0 JSON, snapshot-replace (overwrite=True) => idempotent, and SHA-SKIPs an unchanged reload (compares the freshly-computed source SHA-256 to the last successful INGEST_RUNS.SHA256 for this source_id; equal => no warehouse write). Daily cadence. ~tiny. The reference auto-ACQUIRE recipe.

fed_usgs_earthquakes.note: DISABLED: usgs_quake_backfill.py TRUNCATEs then reloads ALL quakes since 2010 every run (a narrow window would DELETE history). Needs a delta-refresh loader before it is safe to automate. Left here as the disabled-recipe example.
